internal_anchors.details: All 194 unique #anchor hrefs in docs/index.html resolve to existing id attributes. test.js Section 3 (Internal Links) also passed (3452/0 across all suites). 12 unique showTab() targets all map to existing tab-content divs.

tab_structure.details: 12 tab-content divs in canonical order overview→evaluate→model→selftest→wins→pages→killshots→timestamp→predictions→falsify→ai→refs. Each has exactly one section-nav block. Chain is continuous, prev/next linkage validated end-to-end. First (overview) prev=null, last (refs) next=null.

external_links.details: Sampled 5 representative data-source domains (NOAA, NASA, ESA, INTERMAGNET, OpenTimestamps). All resolved 200 OK except two INTERMAGNET URLs (www.intermagnet.org and intermagnet.github.io) which returned 'Redirect was cancelled' — likely a fetcher-policy quirk, since the bare intermagnet.org host returns 200 OK with the same site content. Not a real broken-link issue but worth a follow-up next run. 81 non-citation external URLs in scope; doi.org/publishers excluded per spec.

data_prose_consistency.details: wins.json count = 71 entries. All 6 verdict tally lines render with 'Verdict: N of 71 claims' in HTML aria-labels (Refuted by Data: 9, Self-Contradicted: 14, Misleading: 22, Std Model Explains: 18, Not Demonstrated: 4, Unfalsifiable: 4). new_in_v51 = 32. uncounted-failures.json: 10 total entries split into 4 silent (dome_status_current set) + 6 acknowledged-bucket (no dome_status_current). The scorecard's 'Acknowledged Failures: 4' card is keyed off {{DOME_CLAIMED_FAILURES}} (the dome's own count, =4), not {{ACKNOWLEDGED_FAILURES}}. {{ACKNOWLEDGED_FAILURES}} renders as 6 elsewhere in prose, matching the filtered 'no dome_status_current' bucket. Build reproducibility: ran `node build.js html` against committed docs/index.html — diff is 0 bytes. No unfilled {{TOKEN}} placeholders in HTML.

win_detail_consistency.details: All 71 WIN ids in wins.json have matching id="winNNN" anchors in HTML (incl. winNNN where NNN includes letter suffix like 057b/058b). 8 detail_groups validated: WIN-004/005, WIN-007/022, WIN-008/009, WIN-010/025/068, WIN-011/012, WIN-027/057/069, WIN-040-043+059-061+063 (also includes 057b — minor: group label string omits 057b but content is consistent), WIN-045/046/049/050/051. All 71 wins have non-empty tldr_evidence and tldr_verdict fields.

win_number_alignment.details: Fetched john09289.github.io/predictions/wins.html. Dome site lists 69 distinct WIN numbers (001-069) in headings, with 3 number collisions (different titles for same WIN-NNN in different sections): WIN-057 (Two-Zone Disc Topology vs SAA Simultaneous Acceleration), WIN-058 (Bedrock Induction Ratio vs Unified Angular Coordinate), WIN-062 (Tesla Longitudinal Wave Speed = 1.574c vs Tesla Longitudinal Constant 1.574c — same claim, slight wording variation). Our review's 71 entries cover all 69 dome numbers, plus WIN-057b (Global cavity re-pressurization / SAA synchronous decay) and WIN-058b (Bedrock Induction Ratio κ = 1.677) which explicitly capture the two dome collisions where titles differ substantively. WIN-062 collision is wording-only and our single entry covers it. No untracked collisions, no missing WINs, no untracked aliases.

progressive_disclosure.details: ps-summary count: 116, ps-tldr count: 116 (matched). ks-summary count: 227, ks-tldr count: 227 (matched). 0 empty ps-tldr or ks-tldr paragraphs in HTML. All 14 is_genuinely_prospective predictions in predictions.json have a tldr field. 3 of the 12 'prospective but not is_genuinely_prospective' entries lack a tldr (PRED-R002, PRED-094, PRED-TESLA-002) — informational only; spec wording requires tldr on genuinely prospective predictions.

theme_color_drift.details: 0 hardcoded light-theme background colors (#fff, #f8f9fa, #fafafa, #f5f5f5, #eeeeee, #e0e0e0, named 'white') in inline styles in data/sections.json. Theme variables consistently used.

discoverability.details: docs/llms.txt: present, 5493 bytes, references funwithscience.net/dome-model-review and dome model by name; counts mention '69 dome claims (V51.1)' which is the dome's claimed count, not our wins.json length (71). docs/sitemap.xml: valid XML, urlset root, 2 loc entries (main page + llms.txt). docs/robots.txt: contains Allow: / and Sitemap: directive. data/uncounted-failures.json: schema OK, 10 entries, all have id (FAIL-NNN) + dome_ref + dome_label + what_actually_happened, no duplicate IDs. Meta tags in docs/index.html <head>: og:title ✓, og:description ✓, name=description ✓, ld+json ClaimReview ✓. Minor doc drift: in data/sections.json the {{ACKNOWLEDGED_FAILURES}} placeholder description reads 'Count of entries in uncounted-failures.json' (would imply 10) but the build code substitutes the filtered 'no dome_status_current' subset (=6). Documentation-only mismatch; rendered output is intentional and consistent.

expansion_tracker.details: next_id=249 > max_id=248 ✓ (no collision risk). One ID gap at EXP-052 — single ancient gap on a 247-item tracker, no functional impact (next_id correct). 0 phantom entries, 0 stale pending items. All 237 EXP-NNN files in monitor/analyst/expansions/ map to tracker entries (no orphans). Non-EXP files in expansions/ (ISS-*, MODE5-*, PRED-assessment-*) are analyst Mode 1b/2b/MODE5 outputs and don't require tracker entries.

workspace_only_files.details: 1 MAJOR: monitor/curmudgeon/reviews/WIN-013.c4.json — workspace-only, 20590 bytes, mtime 2026-04-25T07:34Z. This is a real curmudgeon c4 review (current_verdict_holds:true, 0.85 confidence) generated less than an hour ago. Not yet in git; at risk of silent loss if workspace recycles. Workspace-sync should pick it up on next run, but flag now. 2 cleanup items: monitor/curmudgeon/reviews/WIN-067.c3.json.build.js (0-byte stray, mtime 2026-04-21, likely build-script artifact written to wrong path), monitor/analyst/expansions/EXP-202.json.bak (21964-byte backup of a tracked file — clutter). 7 workspace-only top-level analyst .txt/.md files (GLOBE-AUDIT-EXECUTIVE-SUMMARY.txt, globe-dependencies-inventory.txt, latest-analysis-summary.txt, origin-theory.md, python-files-inventory.txt, wgs84-dependency-audit.md, wgs84-detailed-findings.txt) — likely transient analyst scratch outputs; check whether any contain canonical findings that should be committed.

section_new_priority_queue_coupling.details: 0 expansion-tracker items in coupled categories (section-new, category-proposal-writeup) currently pending. Phase 1 Change 1.7 invariant satisfied vacuously.

workspace_sync_skip_log.details: 0 direction-violation skips in last 24h. 19 mtime-guard skips (informational, normal operation): CAT-005.json (4x), expansion-tracker.json (3x), EXP-211-redesign-proposal.json (4x), WIN-067.c6.json (4x), daily-report-2026-04-14T23-13.json (4x). Mtime-guard pattern = git copy is newer than workspace, expected for files multiple agents touch.

digest_coverage.details: 404 review files in monitor/curmudgeon/reviews/, 219 unique base IDs (latest cycle each), 405 entries in processed-reviews.json, 0 unprocessed. All curmudgeon reviews picked up by the digest pipeline. Note: digest's own under-coverage warning ('209 processed reviews have holes with no corresponding open-issues entry') is a separate finding for the decider, not within this agent's scope.

broken_review_files.details: Regenerated digest via node build-scripts/digest-reviews.js (exit 0). pending-digest.json errors[] is empty — 0 review files in parse-error limbo.

claude_md.details: All 12 spot-checked file paths in CLAUDE.md exist (monitor/prompts/{poller,analyst,curmudgeon,decider,structure-integrity,tinker,social,workspace-sync}.md, data/{wins,sections,uncounted-failures,predictions}.json). No hardcoded counts that should be query commands — '69 confirmed predictions' on L7 is the dome's external claim (not our data), and other numerics are dates, schedule times, or issue IDs (PROP-009, SHA-256). Schedule table descriptive only — full cron-vs-doc diff requires scheduled-tasks tool access (out of scope for this run).

build_reproducibility.details: Cloned repo to /tmp/dome-integrity-clone, ran `npm install --silent` and `node build.js html`. Resulting docs/index.html is byte-identical to the committed docs/index.html on origin/main (git diff returns 0 lines). Build is reproducible.

issues_found.description: Workspace-only curmudgeon review WIN-013.c4.json (20590 bytes, generated 2026-04-25T07:34Z) is not committed to git. At risk of silent data loss if workspace recycles before workspace-sync runs.
issues_found.suggested_fix: Workspace-sync should pick this up on its next run. If it doesn't, manually `cp` from FUSE to /tmp clone and commit.

issues_found.description: 0-byte stray file monitor/curmudgeon/reviews/WIN-067.c3.json.build.js (mtime 2026-04-21) — likely a build-script artifact written with the wrong filename. Not a real review, no findings to lose.
issues_found.suggested_fix: Delete via the workspace's tolerated-overwrite mechanism, or leave — it's content-free clutter.

issues_found.description: Backup file monitor/analyst/expansions/EXP-202.json.bak (21964 bytes) duplicates the tracked EXP-202.json. Treated as workspace-only debris — not data at risk.
issues_found.suggested_fix: Remove the .bak; it predates the canonical EXP-202.json by 4 minutes (Apr 22 05:01 vs 05:05) and is superseded.

issues_found.description: 7 workspace-only .txt/.md files at the root of monitor/analyst/ (GLOBE-AUDIT-EXECUTIVE-SUMMARY.txt, globe-dependencies-inventory.txt, latest-analysis-summary.txt, origin-theory.md, python-files-inventory.txt, wgs84-dependency-audit.md, wgs84-detailed-findings.txt). Unclear which (if any) contain canonical analyst findings vs transient scratch.
issues_found.suggested_fix: Audit each — commit anything canonical, delete the rest. Monitor/analyst/ root is not in OWNED_BY_GIT, so workspace-sync won't touch these.

issues_found.description: Single ID gap at EXP-052 in monitor/analyst/expansion-tracker.json (items 1..248 with 247 present). next_id=249 is correct so no collision risk. Likely an old skipped allocation.
issues_found.suggested_fix: No action required — informational. Tracker integrity invariants all hold.

issues_found.description: In data/sections.json the {{ACKNOWLEDGED_FAILURES}} placeholder description reads 'Count of entries in uncounted-failures.json' (which would render 10) but the build substitutes the filtered subset where dome_status_current is unset (=6). The render is intentional; only the description string is misleading.
issues_found.location: data/sections.json (placeholder reference table) and build-scripts/generate-html.js L1233
issues_found.suggested_fix: Update the placeholder description to 'Count of acknowledged-bucket entries (those without dome_status_current) in uncounted-failures.json'.

issues_found.description: https://www.intermagnet.org/ and https://intermagnet.github.io/ both return 'Redirect was cancelled' through the integrity fetcher. The bare https://intermagnet.org/ host returns 200 OK with identical content, suggesting these are fetcher-policy redirect quirks, not real broken links.
issues_found.suggested_fix: Optionally normalize all intermagnet references to https://intermagnet.org/ (no www, no github.io subdomain) to avoid the redirect dance. Defer until a real user hits this.

issues_found.description: Detail group label 'WIN-040-043, 059-061, 063' includes WIN-057b in its content but doesn't list 057b in the label string. Label is descriptive only, not used in any matching/wiring, so no rendering issue.
issues_found.suggested_fix: Either rename the group label to include 057b explicitly, or leave as-is — purely cosmetic.

issues_found.description: 3 prospective predictions without tldr field: PRED-R002, PRED-094, PRED-TESLA-002. None are flagged is_genuinely_prospective, so spec invariant is satisfied. Flagged for analyst awareness.
issues_found.suggested_fix: If any of these are upgraded to is_genuinely_prospective, add a tldr at the same time.

summary: Site is structurally sound. Build reproduces byte-for-byte from data/wins.json + data/sections.json. All 194 internal anchors resolve, 12-tab section-nav chain validates end-to-end, all 71 WINs in wins.json have matching winNNN HTML anchors and align with the dome site's 69 numbers (our 057b/058b explicitly capture the dome's two title-collision sub-claims). No broken external data-source links, no parse-errored curmudgeon reviews, no unprocessed reviews in the digest pipeline. Workspace-sync skip log shows 0 direction violations in 24h. ONE MAJOR FINDING: an unsynced curmudgeon review WIN-013.c4.json sits on the workspace only (20KB, generated ~30 minutes before this run); the next workspace-sync should pick it up but it represents data-at-risk until then. One moderate finding on 7 uncategorized workspace-only analyst .txt/.md files at monitor/analyst/ root. One stale ID gap at EXP-052 (purely cosmetic; next_id is correct). Several minor cleanup items (a 0-byte stray, a .bak file, a placeholder docstring drift, a couple of intermagnet redirect quirks). Overall_status='warn' driven by the workspace-only review file.
